summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. The picture continues to be shaped by external security shocks (Russia’s continuing long‑range strike campaign against Ukraine) and domestic sensitivity to energy and disruption risks.

For the UK, the most immediate near-term disruption risk remains planned industrial action on the London Underground from 21–24 April, with TfL warning of significant disruption and no service on several key segments. Separately, the cyber baseline remains elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed perimeter appliances (Citrix NetScaler), and the terrorism threat level remains SUBSTANTIAL.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Energy-price sensitivity remains a key UK risk channel: Brent is around $90/bbl, keeping fuel and inflation expectations sensitive to external shocks even as the regulated energy price cap is lower for April–June.
drivers: Brent crude oil around 90.38 USD/bbl (Trading Economics snapshot, updated April 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil
ONS: CPI inflation was 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (above target). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year for a typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit (down 6.6% vs prior quarter). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security risk remains elevated due to the continuing high-intensity Russia–Ukraine war and long-range strike activity; UK domestic terrorism risk remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters photo report: Russia launched its deadliest attack of the year so far on Kyiv and other cities overnight on 16 April (killing at least 17). https://www.reuters.com/pictures/russia-launches-deadliest-attack-kyiv-this-year-2026-04-16/
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No evidence reviewed indicates acute UK institutional instability; crisis-management posture continues to focus on monitoring external shocks and domestic economic impacts.
drivers: GOV.UK: COBR(M) meeting (23 Mar 2026) focused on domestic economic impacts of the Middle East situation and potential anti-profiteering measures. https://www.gov.uk/government/news/readout-cobrm-iran-economic-and-domestic-impacts-23-march-2026

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Current England flood alerts are present but not unusually high in the available national snapshot; no imminent major national hazard signal was identified in the sources reviewed.
drivers: GOV.UK flooding service: 14 flood alerts were listed on 19 April 2026 (flooding is possible). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Disruption risk remains elevated due to planned London Underground strike action from 21–24 April, with TfL warning of significant disruption and no service on key lines/sections.
drivers: TfL: RMT Tube drivers plan strike action from 12:00 Tue 21 Apr to 12:00 Fri 24 Apr, with significant disruption expected and no service on the Piccadilly and Circle lines (plus specific Central/Metropolitan sections). https://tfl.gov.uk/info-for/media/press-releases/2026/april/customers-urged-to-check-before-they-travel-ahead-of-strike-action-next-week

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed edge appliances (Citrix NetScaler), consistent with continued exposure to exploitation of internet-facing services.
drivers: NCSC: urges UK organisations to take immediate action on Citrix NetScaler vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368 and install updated versions as soon as possible. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent crude oil at 90.38 USD/bbl (updated April 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Feb 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose by 3.0% in the 12 months to February 2026 (unchanged from January). https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/february2026

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (down 6.6% vs £1,758 for 1 Jan–31 Mar 2026). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 19 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Social Stability drivers updated to use TfL’s official strike-service impact details for 21–24 April.
Natural Hazards updated with a national snapshot of current England flood alerts (14 listed on GOV.UK).
priority_watch_items: Transport disruption: monitor London Underground strike impacts (21–24 April) and any wider industrial-action spillover.
Energy spillovers: watch for renewed oil-price spikes feeding into UK fuel prices and inflation expectations.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation/patching progress for Citrix NetScaler and watch for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further large-scale Russia–Ukraine strike waves and any impacts on energy/commodity infrastructure.
Flooding/Weather: monitor EA flood updates and Met Office warnings for any rapid shift into a higher-impact event.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, GOV.UK, TfL) and Reuters for conflict context; Trading Economics is used for a Brent snapshot. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

England flood-warning counts were not clearly extractable from the GOV.UK flooding homepage during this run; only the flood alert count was visible in the extracted view. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
